evidence: .husky/ directory present with commit-msg, pre-commit, pre-push hooks
gates.expectation: Husky or Lefthook is configured for the repository.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — husky is installed and wired via the prepare script.

evidence: .husky/pre-commit runs `pnpm exec biome check --staged --no-errors-on-unmatched .` (check includes format)
NO lint-staged configuration block in package.json and no .lintstagedrc file
gates.expectation: Prettier or Biome formats staged files via lint-staged.
gates.gap: Formatting of staged files is achieved through `biome check --staged` invoked directly in the pre-commit hook, not through a lint-staged configuration block. The baseline signal `lint-staged configuration block` does not resolve. `biome check` reports formatting issues but, without `--write`/`--apply`, does not auto-fix them on commit.
gates.remediation: Either accept the direct `biome check --staged` approach as an intentional deviation, or add a lint-staged block mapping staged files to `biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched` so formatting is applied rather than only reported.

evidence: biome.json linter.enabled = true with recommended + custom rules
.husky/pre-commit runs `pnpm exec biome check --staged ...` (check includes lint)
NO lint-staged block invoking the linter
gates.expectation: ESLint or Biome lints staged files via lint-staged, with --fix allowed.
gates.gap: Linting of staged files runs via `biome check --staged` directly in the hook rather than through a lint-staged block, so the baseline's `lint-staged block invoking it` signal does not resolve. The lint does run on staged files (good), but `--write`/`--fix` is not passed, so fixable lint issues are reported, not auto-applied.
gates.remediation: Treat the direct `biome check --staged` invocation as an intentional, simpler-than-lint-staged design, or migrate to a lint-staged block with `biome check --write` to allow safe auto-fixes.

evidence: .husky/pre-commit runs `pnpm typecheck` which is `tsc --noEmit` over the WHOLE project, not staged files
gates.expectation: tsc --noEmit (or equivalent) runs against staged TypeScript files.
gates.gap: A type check does run on every commit, but it is a full-project `tsc --noEmit`, not scoped to staged TypeScript files (no `tsc-files` or equivalent). The intent — block type errors before commit — is satisfied, but the staged-scoping signal does not resolve, and the full check is slower than a staged check on large repos.
gates.remediation: Acceptable as-is for a small template (full typecheck is fast and more correct). For larger projects, consider `tsc-files` in a lint-staged block to scope the type check to staged files, accepting the known caveat that staged-only type checking can miss cross-file regressions.

evidence: commitlint.config.ts extends @commitlint/config-conventional with type-enum and subject-case rules
gates.expectation: commitlint enforces Conventional Commits on the subject line.
gates.gap: None. PR-title enforcement for squash merges is not covered (out of baseline scope) but worth a later follow-up.
gates.remediation: No action — commitlint is fully wired. Optional: add a CI step validating squash-merge PR titles.

evidence: gitleaks runs ONLY in CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml security job)
gates.expectation: gitleaks (protect) or detect-secrets runs against staged content before commit.
gates.gap: There is no staged-content secret scan in the pre-commit hook. gitleaks exists but runs only in CI (gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v3), so a secret can be committed and pushed locally and is only caught after it reaches the server.
gates.remediation: Add `gitleaks protect --staged --redact` (or detect-secrets) as a pre-commit step so secrets are blocked before they enter local history, complementing the existing CI scan.

evidence: .husky/pre-push present and executable, wired by husky
gates.expectation: A pre-push hook exists and is wired into the hook runner.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — pre-push hook exists and is managed by husky.

evidence: NO `pnpm typecheck` / `tsc --noEmit` invocation in the pre-push hook
typecheck DOES run in pre-commit and in CI, but not in pre-push
gates.expectation: tsc --noEmit runs over the entire project at pre-push.
gates.gap: The pre-push hook does not run a full type check. By design the repo runs the full `tsc --noEmit` at pre-commit instead. The baseline's pre-push type-check signal does not resolve, but the gap is partly mitigated because pre-commit already runs the full project typecheck (and CI re-runs it).
gates.remediation: If the pre-commit full typecheck is considered sufficient, document the deviation. Otherwise add `pnpm typecheck` to .husky/pre-push so a push is blocked even when commits were made with --no-verify bypassed elsewhere.

evidence: NO `biome lint`/`biome check`/`eslint .` invocation in the pre-push hook
gates.expectation: The project's lint command runs the whole codebase and fails on warnings.
gates.gap: The pre-push hook runs no whole-codebase lint. Lint runs on staged files at pre-commit and on the full tree in CI (`pnpm check`), but not at pre-push. Additionally, Biome warnings (e.g. noNonNullAssertion: warn) do not fail the build — there is no `--max-warnings=0` equivalent, so warning-level findings never block.
gates.remediation: Add `pnpm check` to .husky/pre-push for a full-tree lint before push, and consider promoting warn-level rules to error (or running Biome with a fail-on-warning flag) so warnings are not silently shippable.

gates.expectation: Vitest or Jest runs the unit-test suite to completion at pre-push.
gates.gap: Minor: the hook runs plain `jest`, not `jest --ci`. The suite runs to completion, but the `--ci` flag (no snapshot writes, deterministic CI behaviour) is not used locally. Coverage thresholds are NOT enforced at pre-push (only in CI via `test:coverage`).
gates.remediation: No blocking action. Optionally pass `--ci` and/or run `pnpm test:coverage` at pre-push so the 80% thresholds are enforced before push, not only in CI.

evidence: NO `vite build` / `pnpm build` invocation in the pre-push hook
build DOES run in CI (build job) and indirectly during `pnpm e2e`? — no: pre-push e2e boots the Vite DEV server, not a production build
gates.expectation: The production build command runs and exits cleanly at pre-push.
gates.gap: The pre-push hook does not run `pnpm build`. The Playwright pre-push run boots the Vite dev server (playwright.config.ts webServer = `pnpm dev`), so a broken production build is not caught until CI's dedicated build job.
gates.remediation: Add `pnpm build` to .husky/pre-push for a production build smoke test, or document that the CI build job is the intended gate and accept the later feedback.

gates.expectation: At least one workflow definition exists.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — three workflows are present and parse cleanly.

evidence: ci.yml quality job: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile → pnpm typecheck → pnpm check
ci.yml e2e job: playwright install + pnpm e2e
every job does a fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
gates.expectation: The workflow installs dependencies fresh and runs type-check, lint, unit tests, and build.
gates.gap: None — CI is in fact stricter than pre-push: it runs typecheck, full lint (`pnpm check`), coverage-gated tests, build, and e2e on a clean checkout, covering the pre-push gaps noted above.
gates.remediation: No action — clean-checkout re-run is comprehensive.

evidence: ci.yml e2e job runs `pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium` then `pnpm e2e` (playwright test e2e/app.spec.ts)
gates.expectation: Playwright or Cypress runs against a build of the application.
gates.gap: Minor: in CI the e2e job boots the Vite dev server via playwright.config webServer (no E2E_BASE_URL set in ci.yml), so e2e runs against dev, not a production build. The StyleProof workflow does run against the production preview build separately.
gates.remediation: No blocking action. Optionally set E2E_BASE_URL against a `pnpm preview` production server in the ci.yml e2e job to exercise the built artifact, as the StyleProof workflow already does.

evidence: jest.config.ts coverageThreshold.global = { branches: 80, functions: 80, lines: 80, statements: 80 }
ci.yml test job runs `pnpm test:coverage` (jest --coverage), which fails when thresholds are not met
gates.expectation: The test runner is configured with a coverage threshold and the workflow fails when it is not met.
gates.gap: None. Note: App.tsx and main.tsx are excluded from collectCoverageFrom, so the 80% gate measures library/component code, not the app shell.
gates.remediation: No action. Optionally narrow the coverage exclusions if the app shell grows logic worth covering.

evidence: NO bundle-size step in any workflow
gates.expectation: A bundle-size check runs and fails when the budget is exceeded.
gates.gap: There is no bundle-size budget. A dependency or change that bloats the shipped bundle will pass all gates undetected.
gates.remediation: Add `size-limit` with a `.size-limit.json` budget and a CI step running `pnpm size-limit`, or wire `@size-limit/preset-app` against the Vite `dist/` output.

evidence: NO accessibility step in any workflow
Playwright e2e tests exist but do not invoke an a11y assertion library
gates.expectation: Automated accessibility checks run against components or pages.
gates.gap: No automated accessibility checks run in CI. For a user-facing React app this means a11y regressions ship unflagged.
gates.remediation: Add `@axe-core/playwright` and assert `AxeBuilder` results inside the existing Playwright e2e run, or add `pa11y-ci` as a dedicated CI step.

evidence: secret scan (gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v3) also present in the same job
gates.expectation: A vulnerability scanner runs against the lockfile.
gates.gap: None for the audit signal. No Dependabot configuration (`.github/dependabot.yml` absent), so there is no automated dependency-update PR flow — vulnerabilities are detected but updates are manual.
gates.remediation: No blocking action — pnpm audit gates high-severity vulns. Optionally add `.github/dependabot.yml` to automate dependency-update PRs.

evidence: NO license-check step in any workflow
gates.expectation: A license check runs and enforces an allow-list or deny-list.
gates.gap: There is no license-compliance gate. A transitive dependency under a copyleft or otherwise-disallowed license could be introduced without any signal.
gates.remediation: Add `license-checker-rseidelsohn` (or `license-compliance`) with an allow-list and a CI step that fails on disallowed licenses.

evidence: NO `lhci autorun` step in any workflow
gates.expectation: Lighthouse CI runs against a built version and asserts thresholds (recommended for user-facing apps, flagged not always required).
gates.gap: No Lighthouse CI. Performance, PWA, SEO, and best-practice regressions on this user-facing app are not measured. The StyleProof visual gate covers computed-style diffs but not Lighthouse performance budgets.
gates.remediation: Recommended (not strictly required): add `@lhci/cli`, a `lighthouserc.json` with assertion thresholds, and a CI step running `lhci autorun` against the `pnpm preview` build.
